symptom: a years-old leg fracture that never stopped draining and a small skin tract that still weeps
hook: a dense fragment was floating inside a hollowed-out pocket ringed by a thick wall of bone
whatYouSee: A dense floating fragment of dead bone sitting inside a hollowed-out cavity ringed by thickened living bone.
whyItMatters: Infection cut the blood supply to a piece of bone so that fragment died in place while the bone around it kept building a thick wall to contain it.
treatment: Surgeons cut out the dead bone and clean the cavity and then a long course of antibiotics clears the infection.
takeaway: Bone can die and stay right where it was.
igOptions: A. Chronic osteomyelitis with a sequestrum

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a years-old leg fracture that never stopped draining and a small skin tract that still weeps.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a dense fragment was floating inside a hollowed-out pocket ringed by a thick wall of bone.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Chronic osteomyelitis with sequestrum

👀 What you see:
A dense floating fragment of dead bone sitting inside a hollowed-out cavity ringed by thickened living bone.

🦴 Why it matters:
Infection cut the blood supply to a piece of bone so that fragment died in place while the bone around it kept building a thick wall to contain it.

💊 Treatment:

Surgeons cut out the dead bone and clean the cavity and then a long course of antibiotics clears the infection.

laypersonQuestion: Why is there a floating piece of bone inside the leg?
seedHint: Look at what is trapped inside that hollowed pocket and ask yourself if it belongs there.
